_note: This hook is the authoritative enforcement source for dependency audit commands. The steering file (supply-chain-security.md) intentionally does not duplicate this command list.
name: Dependency Audit on Change
description: Run vulnerability audit when build/dependency files are modified

then.command: echo 'Dependency file modified — running audit...' && { npm audit --audit-level=high 2>/dev/null && echo '[npm] audit passed' ; } || { pip-audit 2>/dev/null && echo '[pip] audit passed' ; } || { poetry audit 2>/dev/null && echo '[poetry] audit passed' ; } || { cargo audit 2>/dev/null && echo '[cargo] audit passed' ; } || { bundler-audit check --update 2>/dev/null && echo '[bundler] audit passed' ; } || { govulncheck ./... 2>/dev/null && echo '[go] audit passed' ; } || { echo 'ERROR: No audit tool found. Install the appropriate tool for your ecosystem:'; echo '  Node.js  -> npm is built-in (npm audit)'; echo '  Python   -> pip install pip-audit'; echo '  Poetry   -> poetry audit (built-in)'; echo '  Rust     -> cargo install cargo-audit'; echo '  Ruby     -> gem install bundler-audit'; echo '  Go       -> go install golang.org/x/vuln/cmd/govulncheck@latest'; exit 1; }
